checks.detail: Perf is multiplied by correctness_rate, so any incorrect/regressing solution collapses both subscores toward 0.
Correctness compares HTML output to CommonMark reference and perf is wall-clock vs cmark, both measure the deliverable not a specific algorithm.
Two dimensions (correctness, performance) with performance gated multiplicatively on correctness so neither can be maxed in isolation while ignoring the other.
Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail nearly all 99 cases, dropping correctness to ~0 and dragging the gated perf term to ~0 as well.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace as root, reads only CLI counters and a root-written failed-cases file, never imports or executes agent code.
Both success and --fail paths write reward.json with score, subscores list, and additional_data, and reward.txt with a single float.

reward_design.formula: composite = 0.5 * correctness_rate + 0.5 * (perf_raw * correctness_rate); per-file perf = min(1.0, (cmark_median * 5.0) / agent_median); correctness_rate = (visible_pass + hidden_pass) / total
reward_design.formula_explanation: Weighted sum of correctness and performance with performance multiplicatively gated by correctness so fast-but-wrong converters cannot harvest half the score.

components.name: correctness
components.metric: fraction of visible+hidden CommonMark cases whose HTML output exactly matches the reference

components.name: performance
components.metric: mean of per-file min(1, 5 * cmark_median / agent_median) across perf corpus

dimension_analysis.name: correctness
dimension_analysis.notes: 99 cases (73 visible + 26 hidden) covering lists, block quotes, loose/tight, lazy continuation, nesting; spec-exact match required.

dimension_analysis.name: performance
dimension_analysis.notes: Only one perf file (perf_nested_lists.md) so perf signal is noisier than ideal, but 5x cmark threshold is generous and gating by correctness prevents standalone gaming.

notes: Consider adding 1-2 more perf inputs (e.g. flat long list, deep block-quote nesting) so a single pathological file does not dominate the perf subscore.
